purpose: DebugTunnel end-to-end generation canaries that verify native macOS AIWizard apps use macOS Lego components and pass playground build/run signals.

cases.goal: Build a polished native macOS Wordle Clone using the Native macOS Lego components. It must use a five-letter grid, physical keyboard input, on-screen keyboard with ENTER and DELETE, offline word list, status feedback, and launch smoke-test stdout. Mandatory: copy the selected macOS components verbatim, include mchatai-macos-components-used.json for recipe.native-word-game, and keep app-specific SwiftUI in separate glue files.

Build a polished native macOS NYT Mini Crossword Clone using the Native macOS Lego components. It should have a 5x5 crossword grid, numbered starts, across/down clue list, keyboard entry, delete, selected-cell highlighting, active-clue highlighting, timer, and solved feedback. Mandatory: copy selected macOS components verbatim, include mchatai-macos-components-used.json for recipe.native-crossword, and keep SwiftUI view glue separate from copied components.

Build a polished native macOS Asteroids Clone using the Native macOS Lego components. It should have vector ship rotation, thrust inertia, bullets, asteroid splitting, lives, score, best score, keyboard controls, and a crisp retro HUD. Mandatory: copy selected macOS components verbatim, include mchatai-macos-components-used.json for recipe.arcade-asteroids, and keep rendering/input glue separate from copied engine files.

Build a polished native macOS Pong Clone using the Native macOS Lego components. It should have paddles, ball physics, score to 7, keyboard controls, reset/pause controls, a crisp arcade HUD, and launch smoke-test stdout. Mandatory: copy selected macOS components verbatim, include mchatai-macos-components-used.json for recipe.arcade-pong, and keep rendering/input glue separate from copied engine files.

Build a polished native macOS BlackJack Clone using the Native macOS Lego components. It should have a deck, visible cards, dealer hand, hit, stand, betting chips, bankroll, settlement status, and keyboard shortcuts. Mandatory: copy selected macOS components verbatim, include mchatai-macos-components-used.json for recipe.blackjack, and keep table UI glue separate from copied card engine files.

Build a polished native macOS Texas Hold'em Poker Clone using the Native macOS Lego components. It should use the native Poker golden assembly shape: one human versus four CPU seats, green felt table, dealer button and blinds, chip stacks, per-seat current bets, hidden opponent hole cards, five community cards, pot, Deal/Fold/Check-or-Call/Raise controls, poker hand rankings, settlement log, tournament-style elimination toward heads-up play, and keyboard shortcuts. Mandatory: copy selected macOS components verbatim, include mchatai-macos-components-used.json for recipe.poker, and keep table UI glue separate from copied card evaluator files.

Build a polished native macOS playable Chess Clone using the Native macOS Lego components. It should use the native Chess golden assembly shape: an 8x8 board, rendered centered pieces, selected square, legal move targets, turn prompts, check/checkmate/stalemate messaging, captured pieces, reset, move log, click controls, and a simple Black CPU opponent that replies automatically after White moves. Mandatory: copy selected macOS components verbatim, include mchatai-macos-components-used.json for recipe.chess, and keep SwiftUI board glue separate from copied rules files.

Build a polished native macOS Checkers Clone using the Native macOS Lego components. It should have an 8x8 board, legal move highlights, captures, same-piece double-jump continuation after any chosen capture, a CPU opponent that also completes multi-jumps, king me, turns, reset, a mono visual style, and a clear optional-vs-mandatory jumps rule control so casual play can move non-jumping pieces while jumps are available. Mandatory: copy selected macOS components verbatim, include mchatai-macos-components-used.json for recipe.checkers, and keep SwiftUI board glue separate from copied rules files.
